title: Olivia Rodrigo's New Song Sends a 1980s Band Back to the Charts!
summary: Have you ever heard a song and then rushed to listen to the one it mentions? That's exactly what happened when Olivia Rodrigo dropped her new single, 'drop dead.' It zoomed straight to No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 — her fourth song to do that! But here's the really cool part: in the first verse, Olivia sings about knowing all the words to 'Just Like Heaven' by The Cure, a band from the 1980s. Suddenly, tons of kids (and grown-ups) started streaming The Cure's greatest hits album. It jumped back onto the Billboard 200 chart at No. 93. That's like a time machine powered by music! Olivia didn't just release one version of the song — she put out six: original, acoustic, sped-up, slowed-down, instrumental, and a cappella. All of them counted together for the chart, like stacking six scoops of ice cream on one cone. She even performed it live at Coachella right after it came out. Meanwhile, country singer Ella Langley stayed at No. 1 on the album chart with 'Dandelion,' and Justin Bieber's old songs like 'Baby' are climbing again after his Coachella show. But the sweetest moment? The Cure has never had a No. 1 song in their long career. Now, thanks to a 21-year-old pop star's lyrics, they get a tiny slice of the top spot. Music is full of surprises!
why_it_matters: Music is a bridge across time – a song you love today might lead you to discover a favorite band your parents or grandparents grew up with.

keywords.explanation: A single is one main song released by an artist, often before a full album.
The Billboard Hot 100 is a weekly list that ranks the most popular songs in the United States.
A verse is a section of a song with its own set of lyrics, usually telling part of the story.
A greatest hits album collects an artist's most famous songs from their career.
Streaming means listening to music online without downloading it, often through apps like Spotify.
A cappella means singing without any musical instruments, using only voices.

questions.question: What is the name of Olivia Rodrigo’s new single?
Which band does Olivia Rodrigo mention in the first verse?
How many different versions of the song did Olivia release?
Where did Olivia perform the song live right after it came out?
What chart position did The Cure’s greatest hits album reach again?
Which country singer stayed at No. 1 on the album chart?

background_read: The Cure is a British rock band that became famous worldwide during the 1980s and is known for songs like 'Friday I’m in Love.'
Olivia Rodrigo (a pop singer who became a huge star in 2021 with her song 'drivers license') often includes references to other artists in her lyrics.
Billboard charts have tracked music popularity since the 1940s, and streaming now helps older songs return to the charts when they are mentioned or used in new hits.
Article_Structure: WHO: Olivia Rodrigo, a 21-year-old pop star, and The Cure, a 1980s rock band.
WHAT: Olivia’s new single 'drop dead' hit No. 1 on the chart and made fans stream The Cure’s greatest hits album until it re-entered the Billboard 200 at No. 93.
WHERE: The song was released globally and performed live at the Coachella music festival.
WHY: The song’s lyrics mention The Cure’s 'Just Like Heaven,' sparking curiosity and a tidal wave of new streams for the older band.

perspectives.description: Young listeners are discovering classic bands they never knew before, making music feel like a shared treasure between generations.
Some people think the sudden return of an old album is a fun but short‑lived blip that won’t stay on the charts for long.
Experts notice that streaming often revives old songs when they appear in new hits, showing how today’s technology changes the way charts work.
